name: Hippogriff
primary_sources: Legends of Charlemagne by Thomas Bulfinch
Harry Potter series by J.K. Rowling

visual_attributes.morphology: Front half of an eagle and hind half of a horse
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Eagle's head and wings at the front, horse's body and legs at the back

domain_specific_visual.heraldic_representation: Not used in British heraldic tradition

domain_specific_functional.narrative_role: Used by characters like Ruggiero and Astolfo in Orlando Furioso
domain_specific_functional.symbolic_role: Represents Christ's dual nature in some traditions

relationships.description: Invented by Ariosto in Orlando Furioso
Originally owned by the sorcerer Atlantes
Ruggiero uses the hippogriff to free Angelica

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Imaginary, associated with North Africa in Orlando Furioso
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbol of impossible love and duality
primary_sources: Legends of Charlemagne by Thomas Bulfinch
